what_it_does: Records an image attachment entry that links a channel-memory item in the design-source channel to a specific image asset URI.
observations: YAML frontmatter with id att-c675f684, channel design-source, attachment_type images, target image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-15, and an ISO 8601 added timestamp.
No body content beyond the frontmatter.
standouts: Target uses an image:// URI scheme.
concerns: Target URI is opaque: no dimensions, MIME type, alt text, or checksum.
No indication of which parent memory item this attachment belongs to beyond the channel.
notes: A lightweight, timestamped attachment ledger entry that maps a design-source channel memory item to a specific image asset.
questions: Which exact channel-memory item does this attachment belong to?
What does the image:// URI resolve to, and what format/size is the image?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches the file: it records an image attachment reference.
intention_signals: To catalog and make retrievable the image assets associated with design-source channel memory entries.
novelty: Uses an image:// URI scheme to address the attached asset rather than a filesystem or HTTP URL.
contribution: Provides a stable, timestamped attachment record that links a channel memory item to an image asset, supporting asset retrieval and provenance tracking.
summary_for_embedding: Attachment metadata record att-c675f684 in the design-source channel referencing image asset image://design-source/presentation-15p/p-15.
intention_for_embedding: To associate a channel memory item with a specific image asset for design-source presentation-15p.
